description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller-driven airplane with a straight, mid-mounted wingspan and a robust, cylindrical fuselage. The aircraft features a glazed nose section, likely for navigation or bombardier purposes, and a prominent tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The engines are mounted on the leading edge of the wings, with four propellers visible, suggesting significant power and range. The fuselage is primarily metallic gray with blue accents along the wing edges and tail, and there are visible windows along the cockpit and fuselage sides. The tail section displays a bold marking, possibly an identifier or squadron insignia. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century military bombers, built for durability and long-range missions.
description: A large, metallic gray four-engine propeller airplane with a glazed nose, mid-mounted straight wings, and a single vertical tail fin, accented with blue details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glazed nose, cockpit windows, and all four propeller engines aligned along the straight wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical fuselage, cockpit placement at the front, and the vertical tail fin with a visible marking.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the four engines and propellers mounted on the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the engine nacelles and tail marking.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear configuration, likely retractable, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
